citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
Two rabbits, identified during the previous inspection (March 31, 2026) with animal welfare concerns needing veterinary
care still have not been evaluated by a veterinarian. The licensee tried but was not able to obtain a veterinary
appointment and he stated he had not called the attending veterinarian. At this visit, when asked how the rabbits were
doing, the licensee explained they are fine and no longer needed a veterinarian. During this inspection, the two rabbits
were found in the following persisting and worsening conditions still needing veterinary care:
• The tan and white rabbit was again found with its right ear dropped but now the entire inner surface of the ear was
completely covered with thick flaky gray material. Near the ear canal the skin was swollen and pink. A small hole through
the ear was apparent near the bottom lower ear margin. The back side of this ear had significant fur loss revealing pink
thickened skin with some white crusting along the ear margin and tip. Some areas along the base of the ear, as well as at
the top and bottom edges, had raised black crusty material present.
• The tan rabbit still had abnormal eyes and now had wet matted fur over most of its lower back and approximately a 1”
circular hairless area with black crusty scab material over pink thickened skin. Both eyes were kept mostly closed with
significant hair loss around them. The skin around the right eye was inflamed, reddish pink in appearance with moist
discharge surrounding the entire eye and towards the nose. The skin around the left eye was more reddish brown in color
with some ulcers and crusty black raised material present with even more hair loss above the eye than before. White
cloudy discharge was still present at the left eye.
At the time of inspection, both rabbits were observed hunched over, eyes closed, sitting individually at the edges of the
enclosure. Failing to acquire veterinary care for these rabbits has caused prolonged medical conditions that may result in
undue pain and distress in the rabbits listed above. The licensee must consult with a veterinarian to obtain a treatment
plan for the animals noted above, and then, moving forward, in cooperation with his attending veterinarian, the licensee
must develop appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries.
Correct by: 04/13/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
Additional Inspectors:
STEPHANIE MAYARD, VETERINARY MEDICAL OFFICER n

narrative:
